mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50 percent of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a broad contraction in aggregate demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to baseline with a p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates robust economic activity that outpaces rate hike concerns, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes during tightening cycles yield negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 2.5 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of high-inflation months over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence periods show Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in less than 45 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent annualized confirms economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0 percent correlates with negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that reduces corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average.
